TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == adidiay tabayal == ŚŹįÁĀ£ŚßĎŚ©ÜŤäč tabayal siwalak, amana mukan. caay kaw tali, malecad kya papah ku tabayal atu tali. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika . Alocasia odora (also called night-scented lily, Asian taro or giant upright elephant ear) is a flowering plant native to Eastand Southeast Asia (Japan, China, Indochina, Assam, Bangladesh, Borneo, Taiwan). In Manipur, local name is Hoomu. Alocasia odora (called Ray) can be used as medicine for the treatment of common cold in North Vietnam.
S.PHON: == adidiaj tabajar~…ĺ == ***** tabajar~…ĺ siwar~…ĺak, amana mukan. ¶aaj kaw tar~…ĺi, mar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad kja papańß ku tabajar~…ĺ atu tar~…ĺi. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika . ar~…ĺo ¶asia odora (ar~…ĺso ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd nigńßt-s ¶…ônt…ôd r~…ĺir~…ĺj, asian taro or giant uprigńßt …ôr~…ĺ…ôpńßant …ôar) is a fr~…ĺow…ôriŇč pr~…ĺant nati*…ô to …ôastand soutńß…ôast asia (*apan, ¶ńßina, indo ¶ńßina, assam, baŇčr~…ĺad…ôsńß, born…ôo, taiwan). in manipur, r~…ĺo ¶ar~…ĺ nam…ô is ńßoomu. ar~…ĺo ¶asia odora ( ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd *aj) ¶an b…ô us…ôd as m…ôdi ¶in…ô for tńß…ô tr…ôatm…ônt of ¶ommon ¶or~…ĺd in nortńß *i…ôtnam.
S.FORM: == adidiay tabayal == ŚŹįÁĀ£ŚßĎŚ©ÜŤäč tabayal siwalak, amana mukan. caay kaw tali, malecad kya papah ku tabayal atu tali. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika . Alocasia odora (also called night-scented lily, Asian taro or giant upright elephant ear) is a flowering plant native to Eastand Southeast Asia (Japan, China, Indochina, Assam, Bangladesh, Borneo, Taiwan). In Manipur, local name is Hoomu. Alocasia odora (called Ray) can be used as medicine for the treatment of common cold in North Vietnam.
S.PHON: == adidiaj tabajar~…ĺ == ***** tabajar~…ĺ siwar~…ĺak, amana mukan. ¶aaj kaw tar~…ĺi, mar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad kja papańß ku tabajar~…ĺ atu tar~…ĺi. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika . ar~…ĺo ¶asia odora (ar~…ĺso ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd nigńßt-s ¶…ônt…ôd r~…ĺir~…ĺj, asian taro or giant uprigńßt …ôr~…ĺ…ôpńßant …ôar) is a fr~…ĺow…ôriŇč pr~…ĺant nati*…ô to …ôastand soutńß…ôast asia (*apan, ¶ńßina, indo ¶ńßina, assam, baŇčr~…ĺad…ôsńß, born…ôo, taiwan). in manipur, r~…ĺo ¶ar~…ĺ nam…ô is ńßoomu. ar~…ĺo ¶asia odora ( ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd *aj) ¶an b…ô us…ôd as m…ôdi ¶in…ô for tńß…ô tr…ôatm…ônt of ¶ommon ¶or~…ĺd in nortńß *i…ôtnam.
